content.code:
std::vector v1 { 5 }; // defines a 1 element vector containing value `5`.
std::vector v2 ( 5 ); // defines a 5 element vector where elements are value-initialized.

list: Useresize()when accessing a vector via indexing. This changes the length of the vector so your indices will be valid.Usereserve()when accessing a vector using stack operations. This adds capacity without changing the length of the vector.
Usereserve()when accessing a vector using stack operations. This adds capacity without changing the length of the vector.

content.code: std::vector evens { 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 };
const std::vector d { 1.2, 3.4, 5.6, 7.8 }; // reminder: std::vector can't be constexpr

using namespace std::literals::string_view_literals; // for sv suffix
const std::vector names { "Alex"sv, "Brad"sv, "Charles"sv, "Dave"sv }; // sv suffix needed for CTAD to infer std::string_view
std::vector v { 12 };

content.text: When initializing astd::vectorwith element values, we should use list initialization.

content.code: std::vector<int> v( 12 );

content.text: When initializing astd::vectorwith an initial length, we must use direct initialization. We also must explicitly specify the type template argument since there are no initializers in which to infer the element type from.

list: The computer will pick a word at random and draw an underscore for each letter in the word.The player wins if they guess all the letters in the word before making X wrong guesses (where X is configurable).
The player wins if they guess all the letters in the word before making X wrong guesses (where X is configurable).

The player will guess a single letter.If the player has already guessed that letter, it doesn’t count, and play continues.If any of the underscores represent that letter, those underscores are replaced with that letter, and play continues.If no underscores represent that letter, the player uses up one of their wrong guesses.

If the player has already guessed that letter, it doesn’t count, and play continues.If any of the underscores represent that letter, those underscores are replaced with that letter, and play continues.If no underscores represent that letter, the player uses up one of their wrong guesses.

If any of the underscores represent that letter, those underscores are replaced with that letter, and play continues.If no underscores represent that letter, the player uses up one of their wrong guesses.
If no underscores represent that letter, the player uses up one of their wrong guesses.
The player should know how many wrong guesses they have left.The player should know what letters they have guessed incorrectly (in alphabetical order).
The player should know what letters they have guessed incorrectly (in alphabetical order).

list: We’ll start by defining our list of words and writing a random word picker. You can use the Random.h from lesson8.15 -- Global random numbers (Random.h)to assist.

First define a namespace namedWordList. The starter list of words is: “mystery”, “broccoli” , “account”, “almost”, “spaghetti”, “opinion”, “beautiful”, “distance”, “luggage”. You can add others if you like.Write a function to pick a random word and display the word picked. Run the program several times to make sure the word is randomized.
Write a function to pick a random word and display the word picked. Run the program several times to make sure the word is randomized.

list: Create a class namedSessionthat will be used to store all of the data the game needs to manage in a game session. For now, we just need to know what the random word is.Create a function to display the basic state of the game, where the word is displayed as underscores.Create a function to accept a letter of input from the user. Do basic input validation to filter out non-letters or extraneous input.

Create a function to display the basic state of the game, where the word is displayed as underscores.Create a function to accept a letter of input from the user. Do basic input validation to filter out non-letters or extraneous input.

Keep track of which letters the user has guessed.Display correctly guessed letters.Implement a basic game loop.
Display correctly guessed letters.Implement a basic game loop.
Implement a basic game loop.
